_comment: SAFE DEMO TARGETS ONLY. Hard rule: demo data is the user's OWN public test page, a company/product news page, a public GitHub release page, or a hand-made static evidence HTML. NEVER a real ex, NEVER a real dating-app profile, NEVER a real private account, NEVER any non-consenting private individual. All entries below are synthetic/own-public and clearly labeled. Replace placeholder hosts with your OWN test assets before running the demo; do not substitute a real person's URL.
rules: Only self / brand / public_figure(public-capacity) / safety_evidence(own) targets.
Single public URL, no login, respects robots.txt and rate limits.
No crawling outward from any URL.
If you cannot honestly classify a target into the scope_type enum, it does NOT belong here.

allowed.label: User's own public test page (you control this domain)
allowed.note: Synthetic placeholder. Use a page YOU own/control for the footprint-audit demo.

allowed.label: Company / product news page
allowed.note: Public organizational/brand news. No individual re-identification.

allowed.label: Public GitHub release page (open-source project)
allowed.note: Public release notes of a project/org. robots-permitted, public, single URL.

allowed.label: Hand-made static evidence HTML (about the user themselves)
allowed.note: Synthetic, locally authored evidence sample involving the user. Demonstrates single-item preservation + retention, not monitoring of any third party.

allowed.label: Public figure's OFFICIAL public statement page (public capacity)
allowed.note: Synthetic placeholder for an official/public-capacity page. Never a private individual.
